title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXXXIV. Love And Matrimony.
content:
Bessy Bell and Mary Gray,[*]
            They were two bonny lasses:
        They built their house upon the lea,
            And covered it with rashes.

        Bessy kept the garden gate,
            And Mary kept the pantry:
        Bessy always had to wait,
            While Mary lived in plenty.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Unknown delivers a powerful performance in "Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXXXIV. Love And Matrimony."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
